properties.hs_note_body:
Post-discovery call notes — Acme Corp (11/12)

  • CTO (Mike Johnson) added new hard requirement mid-call: AWS us-east-1 data residency. This came from a compliance audit. Non-negotiable.
  • Analytics lead (Sarah Chen) confirmed budget ~$80K annually, already approved. Has flexibility if ROI case is strong.
  • They're doubling the analytics team Q1 — this creates urgency. Current tool doesn't scale access control.
  • Champion is Sarah. Economic buyer is likely CFO (not on call yet). Need to get CFO intro before legal review.

Risk: procurement cycle could be slow — Sarah mentioned 'legal review' before contract. Push for LOI before end of year.

Post-technical deep-dive (11/19). Jordan confirmed all requirements met: RLS, PII masking, audit log, Splunk integration. Big positive: Sarah proactively asked about 2-year deal — this is a strong buying signal, suggests internal budget conversation has already happened. Action: get pricing out by EOD Friday. Watch out: Mike added SIEM requirement that wasn't on original list — pattern suggests more requirements may surface. Recommend getting a formal requirements sign-off doc before technical spec is shared.

properties.hs_meeting_body: Initial discovery call with Sarah Chen (Analytics Lead) and Mike Johnson (CTO). Agenda: current state pain points, technical requirements, and evaluation criteria.
properties.hs_meeting_outcome: Completed. Strong technical fit. Moved to technical validation. Follow-up: SE deep-dive scheduled for 11/19.

properties.hs_meeting_body: Technical deep-dive with Jordan Lee (Solutions Engineer). Topics: data residency architecture, row-level security implementation, PII masking, and audit log export to SIEM.
properties.hs_meeting_outcome: All technical requirements confirmed met. New requirement surfaced: SIEM audit log export (Splunk) — confirmed supported. Prospect asked about 2-year pricing structure. Next: commercial discussion.
